title: Credentials should not be hard-coded
description: Hard-coding credentials such as passwords, API keys, or tokens in source code is a dangerous practice. It exposes sensitive information to anyone with access to the codebase and increases the risk of accidental leaks or malicious use. Credentials should be stored securely using environment variables or dedicated secret management tools.
recommendation: Never store credentials directly in code. Use secure storage mechanisms and ensure secrets are not committed to version control.
impact: Hard-coded credentials can be easily discovered and exploited, leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, and compliance violations.

examples.good: String password = System.getenv("DB_PASSWORD"); // Compliant: uses environment variable

checks.message: Never store credentials directly in code. Use secure storage mechanisms and ensure secrets are not committed to version control.

original_logic_description: Detect assignments of string literals to variables or fields with names indicating credentials (e.g., password, secret, token, key). Flag any such assignment as a violation.
